name: Fried Okra
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan over medium heat, combine 8 oz canned tomato sauce, 1 small chopped onion, 1 seeded and chopped green bell pepper, 1/8 ts chopped garlic, 3/4 ts olive oil, and 1/8 ts chili powder with 1/8 ts black pepper. Cook for about 15 minutes to blend flavors.
2. In a separate small saucepan over low heat, melt 3/4 tb butter/margarine. Stir in 3/4 tb all-purpose flour; cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Increase the heat to medium and stir in 3/4 cup milk. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally until slightly thickened. Gradually stir in 3/4 cup shredded sharp cheddar cheese, then stir in 4 oz drained chopped green chilies with 1/8 ts each ground red pepper and black pepper. Keep the dipping sauces warm.
3. In a 4-quart saucepan using a deep-fry thermometer, heat vegetable oil to 365 degrees Fahrenheit. Meanwhile, slice stems and tips from okra. In a medium bowl, combine yellow cornmeal, paprika, ground red pepper, salt, hot pepper sauce, and egg beaten with 1 tb water.
4. Coat each piece of okra in the batter mixture. Fry coated okra in small batches until golden brown, about 1 1/2 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon to paper towels to drain. Serve immediately with warm dipping sauces.
